diagnosis: The player spent 180 seconds wandering a large, multi-tiered level without locating the boss or exit, eventually giving up at the spawn point. Two combat deaths occurred in a narrow corridor around x=3200–3400 where enemies (likely the '4'/'8' shooters on row 1) can attack from above with little cover. The trace shows extensive backtracking and long idle periods (e.g., 30+ seconds stationary at x≈3580, y=178), indicating the player was lost, not just challenged. No progress past the upper-right region was made, so the boss/exit placement is effectively unreachable without clearer guidance.

lessons.lesson: When a level spans multiple vertical tiers connected by sparse ladders or gaps, players fail to build a mental map and abandon the search for objectives.
lessons.outcome: player wanders, backtracks extensively, and quits without finding boss/exit

lessons.lesson: Placing ranged enemies on elevated platforms above narrow horizontal corridors creates unavoidable damage zones that feel unfair rather than challenging.
lessons.outcome: repeated combat deaths in same corridor, frustration
